definition: The free mobile app from Meta designed for small businesses to communicate with customers over WhatsApp. It runs on your phone (or up to 5 linked devices), gives you a business profile, and includes basic tools like quick replies, labels, a product catalog, and broadcast lists. No coding or API access required.
target_audience: Solopreneurs, freelancers, and small businesses with low message volume (under ~50 conversations per day). Ideal for people who want to talk to customers on WhatsApp without any technical setup or monthly fees. No developer skills needed.
key_features: Business profile with address, hours, website, and description
Product catalog displayed directly in the chat
Broadcast lists to send the same message to up to 256 saved contacts at once
Quick replies and automated greeting/away messages
Labels for organizing chats and contacts
Link up to 5 devices (1 primary phone + 4 linked devices) on the free plan, or up to 10 devices with Meta Verified subscription
Click-to-chat links and QR codes for easy customer entry
WhatsApp Business Coexistence (2025) allows the same number to connect to Cloud API simultaneously for scaling, while keeping the app for 1:1 chats
Meta Verified subscription option for instant blue tick badge and extra features
limitations: Broadcast lists capped at 256 recipients, and recipients must have your number saved in their contacts to receive the message
No real multi-agent support -- one phone plus linked devices, but no shared inbox, ticket routing, or agent assignment
No CRM or helpdesk integrations (no API access)
Automation is limited to greeting messages, away messages, and quick replies -- no chatbot flows or conditional logic
No message templates or proactive outbound messaging outside the 24-hour customer service window (unlike the API)
Analytics are minimal -- no conversion tracking, no campaign performance data
Cannot send bulk marketing campaigns at scale
Coexistence feature not available in EU/EEA countries, UK, Australia, Japan, and several other regions
pricing: Completely free to download and use. No per-message fees, no subscription. The optional Meta Verified subscription (available in select countries) adds a blue tick badge and expands the linked device limit to 10, starting at roughly $12-21/month depending on the plan tier (Standard, Plus, Premium, Max). Messages sent through the app itself are always free, even if you enable Coexistence with the Cloud API -- only API-side messages incur Meta's per-message charges.
scalability_ceiling: The hard ceiling is the 256-contact broadcast limit and the lack of API access. Once you need to message more than 256 people at once, run multi-agent customer support, integrate with a CRM, or automate workflows beyond simple quick replies, you've outgrown the app. The Coexistence feature (2025) offers a bridge -- you can connect your app number to the Cloud API through a BSP without losing your chat history, effectively letting you scale the API side while keeping the app for casual 1:1 chats.
setup_complexity: 1 out of 5. Download the app from the App Store or Google Play, verify your phone number via SMS or call, fill in your business profile, and you're live. A WordPress site owner can be up and running in under 10 minutes with zero technical knowledge.
migration_complexity: Migrating from the Business App to the Cloud API used to mean deleting your app account and losing all chat history. Since the 2025 Coexistence update, businesses in supported regions can onboard their existing app number to the Cloud API through a BSP without deleting the app. The last ~6 months of chat history is preserved, and both platforms work simultaneously. For unsupported regions (EU/EEA, UK, Australia, etc.), migration still requires registering the number on a new WABA, which means chat history is lost. Number porting itself takes minutes with minimal downtime, but you need to back up conversations manually beforehand. Template messages created on the API side do not carry over from the app (there are none to carry over, since the app doesn't use templates).
data_compliance: The WhatsApp Business App has significant GDPR concerns. It automatically uploads your phone's contact list to WhatsApp servers, which means you're sharing personal data of contacts who never consented to that processing. There is no Data Processing Agreement (DPA) available for the app. For EU-based businesses subject to GDPR, this is a real problem -- multiple European courts have flagged this behavior. There's no way to restrict data hosting to EU servers, and you have no control over metadata processing. The API (through a GDPR-compliant BSP with EU hosting) is the only truly compliant path for businesses handling EU customer data.
verification_path: Two options exist. First, Meta Verified: a paid subscription available directly in the app that grants an instant blue tick badge, starting at around $12-21/month. No notability requirements -- any legitimate business can subscribe. Second, Official Business Account (OBA): the traditional free verification path, but it requires the Cloud API (not available on the app alone), a verified Meta Business Portfolio, 30+ days of account history, and proof of brand notability through 3-5 organic press articles. OBA verification must now be submitted through a BSP. For app-only users, Meta Verified is the only realistic path to the blue tick.
message_template_rules: Not applicable to the Business App. Message templates are an API-only concept. The app lets you send free-form messages to anyone within the 24-hour customer service window, and broadcast messages to saved contacts. There is no template approval process, no category system (Marketing/Utility/Authentication), and no pre-approval workflow. This is both a simplicity advantage and a limitation -- you can't initiate proactive outbound campaigns outside the service window like you can with API templates.
messaging_limits: The app does not have the formal tiered messaging limit system that the API uses (250/day, 1K, 10K, 100K tiers). Instead, limits are informal: broadcast lists cap at 256 recipients who must have your number saved, and WhatsApp's spam detection algorithms will flag or temporarily block your number if you send too many messages too quickly. There is no official daily cap published for 1:1 conversations, but aggressive messaging patterns will trigger anti-spam measures. The ~2 marketing messages per user per day frequency cap that applies to API marketing templates does not formally apply to the app, but spam behavior will still get you blocked.
ai_automation_policy: The app itself only supports basic automation: greeting messages, away messages, and quick replies. There are no chatbot capabilities built in. The January 15, 2026 Meta policy banning general-purpose AI chatbots on WhatsApp primarily targets API-based deployments, but the principle applies across the platform. If you were somehow running an AI assistant through the app (via third-party workarounds), it would need to be a structured, purpose-driven bot (support, bookings, order tracking) rather than an open-domain AI assistant.
quality_risk: The app does not have the formal Green/Yellow/Red quality rating system that the API uses. However, your number can still be reported by users and temporarily or permanently banned by WhatsApp's anti-spam systems. If too many recipients block your number or report your messages as spam, you risk a temporary restriction or permanent ban. Recovery involves appealing through WhatsApp support, but there's no structured quality dashboard or tier downgrade system like the API has.
ads_integration: The Business App supports Click-to-WhatsApp Ads (CTWA) -- you can run Facebook and Instagram ads that open a WhatsApp conversation with your business number. The 72-hour free messaging window applies: when a customer comes in through a CTWA ad, all messages within that window are free (this applies on the API side if you're using Coexistence). However, CAPI (Conversions API) conversion tracking is not available through the app alone -- you need the API and a BSP with CAPI integration to track purchase or lead conversions back to your ad campaigns in Meta Ads Manager.
ecommerce_features: The app includes a built-in product catalog where you can add up to 500 products with images, descriptions, prices, and product codes. Customers can browse the catalog directly in the chat and add items to a cart. However, there is no native WooCommerce or Shopify sync -- you'd need to update the catalog manually. There are no in-chat payment capabilities built into the app (payments via WhatsApp are only available in select countries like India and Brazil). For WordPress/WooCommerce store owners, the catalog is useful for showcasing products but won't sync inventory or orders automatically.
wpchat_relevance: WPChat.com bridges the biggest gap for Business App users on WordPress: it puts a professional WhatsApp chat widget on your site that routes visitors directly into your WhatsApp Business App conversations. Without WPChat, you'd need to manually add click-to-chat links or rely on basic WhatsApp widgets with limited customization. WPChat adds AI-powered FAQs that can answer common questions before a customer even messages you (reducing the load on your single-device setup), Chat Funnels that guide visitors toward purchases or support paths, agent management with availability scheduling, and analytics to track which conversations convert. For app-only users, WPChat essentially acts as the 'front door' on your website that the Business App lacks -- it handles the pre-chat experience that the app can't.
best_for: Solopreneurs and freelancers who handle customer conversations personally
Local businesses (restaurants, salons, repair shops) with a loyal customer base under 256 contacts
Small WordPress site owners who want a WhatsApp presence without any cost or complexity
Businesses just getting started with WhatsApp who want to test the channel before investing in the API
Service providers (consultants, coaches, tutors) who rely on 1:1 messaging
not_suited_for: Businesses that need to send bulk campaigns to more than 256 people, run multi-agent support teams, integrate WhatsApp with a CRM or helpdesk, automate complex workflows, or require GDPR-compliant data processing with a DPA. If you're an ecommerce store with hundreds of daily orders or an agency managing WhatsApp for clients, the app will hit its ceiling fast.
cta_angle: If you're using the WhatsApp Business App on your WordPress site, WPChat.com is the natural next step -- it turns your site into a lead-capturing, question-answering machine that feeds conversations directly into WhatsApp, without needing the API. And when you do outgrow the app, WPChat works with the API path too, so your setup scales with you.
